old_claim.note: Telegraph provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct telegraph prerequisite remains; electric telegraphy became a later command-and-control subject after the scoped staff-college tier was already operating.

source_shape.source_locator: Journal pp. 125-126, section "Phase Two - the Kriegsakademie Era": identifies the early nineteenth-century staff-college tier and dates Prussia's General War School to 1810.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The canonical Prussian staff-college model was established in 1810 as a selective mid-career professional military school.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The local electric-telegraph node is dated to 1837, twenty-seven years after the evidenced college. Telegraphy could alter later staff practice but could not enable creation of an institution already in operation.

ontology_before: military_staff_colleges treated electric telegraphy as a direct enabling prerequisite despite anchoring the institution to a nineteenth-century date copied from telegraph history.
ontology_after: military_staff_colleges is anchored to the 1810 professional-education institution and does not turn later communications tools into founding prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-military-staff-college-scope: telegraph is absent as a direct prerequisite while the staff-college chronology is fixed at 1810.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that the scoped 1810 staff-college institution depended on electric telegraphy rather than merely teaching or using it in later decades.
short_reason: The staff-college tier predates electric telegraphy, so telegraph use in later military planning does not license a founding dependency.
